Home/World of Warcraft/Midnight Expansion Guide/Mythic+ Season 1/Mythic+ Tank Rankings

Mythic+ Tank Rankings: A Tier List for Midnight (Patch 12.0.1 / Midnight)

Last updated on Mar 15, 2026 at 10:26 by Petko 43 comments

From the weakest to the strongest tank specialization, each provide unique character distinction and add value toward shaping the Midnight Season 1 "meta" of Mythic+. The Tank specialization rankings provided for Midnight Season 1 are based on pure tanking strength and group-added-value such as utility, survivability, mobility, damage and self-sustain.

This list will be updated as new tuning and class changes come out over the course of the season.

TABLE OF CONTENTS OF THIS PAGE
  • 1. About the Tier List
  • 2. Mythic+ Tank Tier List for Midnight Season 1
  • 3. Full Tank Class Rankings
1.

About the Tier List

This tier list is NOT FINAL; it will change and will be updated regularly during Midnight Mythic+ Season 1. There are often moments of tier set and class tuning, as well as talent changes and secondary stat scaling that will impact the placement of the specs.

The below tier list is a result of numerous hours of testing on Midnight Beta (max level gear/tier-sets). It will be entirely focused on Mythic+ performance in Midnight Season 1, and is based on empirical evidence more so than simulations or aggregated data analysis, with the ranking based on both my own personal point of view, as well as that of many of my peers.

LAST UPDATED - 15th of March

1.1.

DPS and Healer Class Rankings

If you are interested in our other rankings for Mythic+ Midnight Season 1, please click the links below.

Mythic+ DPS Rankings Mythic+ Healer Rankings

This tier list is entirely based on several factors, such as tank durability, group composition synergy, mobility, damage profile, self-sustain and external party-wide cooldowns.

2.

Mythic+ Tank Tier List for Midnight Season 1

Below, you can find the full rankings for Tank specializations in Midnight Season 1. We encourage you to read further and understand why some classes are placed lower or higher than expected, with several factors taken into consideration.

S
Brewmaster Monk

Brewmaster Monk received its fair share of changes leading up to the Midnight expansion. An intense rotational pruning made it much easier and less button-bloaty, while keeping its core spells limited to 4-6 abilities. Dampen Harm and Diffuse Magic (at least the damage reduction part) were also removed. The core rotation will still rely on Stagger and Purifying Brew, which the spec has always been known for. A much more streamlined rotational approach was taken into consideration for this redesign, and I am personally a big fan of it, as it turned out to be one of the strongest tank options defensively thus far, on top of having one of the highest damage outputs across all tanks.

Brewmaster Monk Guide
A+
Protection Warrior

Protection Warrior received its fair share of changes leading up to the Midnight. Protection Warrior is likely one of the few specs that didn't really get any significant shift in rotation, as the core remains quite similar. The APM requirement for Protection warrior has slightly diminished, partly because of lower haste levels during Season 1 and part of it because of the Anger Management nerfs. With the most recent buffs, it helped elevate and improve its overall performance both offensively and defensively. As every warrior spec, Protection warrior will enjoy several new utility options to help your team!

Protection Warrior Guide
Vengeance Demon Hunter

Vengeance Demon Hunter received a complete rework as we venture into Midnight, with some of the highlights being one of the best Apex talents across all tanks, an improved Metamorphosis, Fel Devastation has no Demonic to follow, Soul Cleave has now been changed to a 6-target soft cap (previously hard-capped) and Fiery Brand received a much needed quality-of-life update. These changes are quite dramatic, and with the target-caps lifted their AoE capabilities improved, on top of also being super tanky. They will retain their great party-wide utility while getting better everywhere else.

Vengeance Demon Hunter Guide
A
Protection Paladin

Protection Paladin received a fair amount of changes leading up to the Midnight expansion, with some of the highlights being: Less defensive value tied to Consecration, Eye of Tyr removed, and Guardian of Ancient Kings will have the option to get an extra stack thanks to Empyrean Authority. Mana management has also become much better, and is only ever an issue if you try to spot-heal your allies instead of using it on yourself. The lack of a solid way to incur cooldown reduction on your major CDs has hit Protection Paladin quite heavily, as currently they are one of the squishiest tanks. Although the damage profile and overall utility package are looking great, a step forward towards improving durability must happen first before we can rank them higher.

Protection Paladin Guide
B
Guardian Druid

Guardian Druid received a bunch of changes for the Midnight expansion. Overall the spec will play a lot less spammy, mostly due to the removal of Thorns of Iron, and have a better optimized tree while retaining its low barrier-to-entry title among the tanks. Additionally, both sustain and durability are doing well, unfortunately after their most recent substantial damage nerfs, they have dropped a few ranks down.

Guardian Druid Guide
Blood Death Knight

Blood Death Knight received a few changes leading up to the Midnight expansion, for example a weaker and shorter Dancing Rune Weapon, on top of getting an RNG DRW from your capstone apex talents, and a significant rotational pruning of the spec. The barrier to entry is arguably the lowest BDK has ever had, as the spec offers a much more streamlined approach compared to before.

Blood Death Knight Guide
3.

Full Tank Class Rankings

3.1.

S

3.1.1.

Brewmaster Monk

Utility Package
Functionality Spells Notes
Core Mystic Touch 5% extra Physical damage for your raid.
Core Leg Sweep 3 sec stun effect.
Optional Tiger's Lust Removes all roots and snares.
Optional Ring of Peace It can be used to help your tank kite and as a AoE disturb effect.
Optional Detox Removes all Poison and Disease effects from the target.
Optional Paralysis Single-target disturb effect & Enrage dispel.
Brewmaster Monk
3.2.

A+Tier

3.2.1.

Protection Warrior

Utility Package
Functionality Spells Notes
Core Rallying Cry Temporary increasing the health of your raid/party. The value can be increased if you talent Battlefield Commander.
Core Battle Shout Extra 5% attack power.
Core Shockwave -
Core Storm Bolt -
Core Intimidating Shout Uncapped AoE Stop.
Optional Javelineer Wrecking Throw or Shattering Throw silences target for 3 sec.
Optional Piercing Howl 70% slow on enemies. If you have talented Battlefield Commander it will also give 30% movement speed bonus for allies.
Optional Champion's Spear Grips all enemies to the location of the spear.
Optional Berserker Shout Removes all fear effects from your group members within 12 yards if you have talented Battlefield Commander.
Optional Spell Reflection Usable in niche situations.
Protection Warrior
3.2.2.

Vengeance Demon Hunter

Utility Package
Functionality Spells Notes
Core Chaos Brand 3% extra Magical damage.
Optional Darkness % chance to avoid all damage from an attack.
Core Sigil of Silence Silences all enemies affected by the Sigil for a short time.
Optional Sigil of Chains All enemies affected by the Sigil are pulled to its center and snared.
Core Chaos Nova -
Optional Imprison Single-target out of combat crowd-control.
Optional Consume Magic Remove 1 beneficial Magic effect.
Core Sigil of Misery AoE disturb effect.
Vengeance Demon Hunter
3.3.

A-Tier

3.3.1.

Protection Paladin

Utility Package
Functionality Spells Notes
Core Avenger's Shield Extra interrupt option that has a follow-up silence effect.
Core Devotion Aura Permanent 3% group/raid-wide damage reduction aura.
Core Lay on Hands Heals a member of your party/raid to full health.
Core Intercession Combat-resurrection.
Core Hammer of Justice -
Optional Blessing of Spellwarding Granting magical damage immunity for 10 sec on a chosen ally.
Optional Blessing of Sacrifice Helps a single member of your raid in a critical situation. This can be further improved by talenting Sacrifice of the Just.
Optional Blessing of Protection Physical immunity, which can help you with some boss encounters.
Optional Blessing of Freedom Immunity to movement impairing effects.
Optional Cleanse Toxins Removes all Poison and Disease effects from the target.
Optional Turn Evil Be able to fear any Undead, Aberration or Demon, which is an additional form of crowd control.
Protection Paladin
3.4.

B-Tier

3.4.1.

Guardian Druid

Utility Package
Functionality Spells Notes
Core Mark of the Wild Increasing the Versatility of your raid/party by 3%.
Optional Innervate Allowing 1 friendly healer to cast spells without spending mana for 8 sec.
Optional Stampeding Roar Increasing the movement speed to all raid members by 60% for 8 sec.
Optional Remove Corruption Removes all Curse and Poison effects from a friendly target.
Core Soothe Dispelling all Enrage Effects from an enemy target.
Optional Hibernate Single-target crowd-control effects against beasts and dragonkins.
Optional Incapacitating Roar / Mighty Bash You have the choice between AoE disturb effect or a single-target stun.
Optional Typhoon AoE knockback.
Optional Mass Entanglement / Ursol's Vortex You have the choice between AoE root effect or AoE wind-of-vortex, pulling all the mobs to one location.
Guardian Druid
3.4.2.

Blood Death Knight

Utility Package
Functionality Spells Notes
Core Anti-Magic Zone Provides extra durability against heavy magical damage.
Core Death Grip Repositions an enemy of your choice.
Core Raise Ally Combat-resurrection.
Optional Blinding Sleet AoE disturb effect.
Optional Gorefiend's Grasp Mass-grip.
Optional Insidious Chill Reduces the auto-attack speed of your target by 5% stacking up 4 times.
Optional Control Undead Mind-control an undead enemy to fight on your side.
Optional Asphyxiate 5 sec single-target stun.
Optional Vestigial Shell Adding Anti-Magic Shell on 2 nearby allies.
Blood Death Knight
4.

ABOUT THE AUTHOR

This guide has been written by Petko, one of the top Mythic+ players of all time. He has accomplished several Rank 1 World & Europe Team and Solo seasonal rankings, with multiple classes over the span of multiple expansions. You can catch his daily streams on Twitch, and find many of his educational videos on YouTube and TikTok.
